steps.title: Identify what needs to be compared
steps.content: The goal is to compare disadvantages—what makes each system harder to use or deploy? From the notes: Wayang Windu's flash steam system needs reservoirs above 180℃ (limiting its applicability since many reservoirs aren't hot enough); Raft River's binary cycle system costs more to maintain. To accomplish the goal, a choice must address both disadvantages, not just one.

steps.title: Match the comparison structure
steps.content:
Choice C identifies Wayang Windu's disadvantage (temperature limitations) and directly contrasts it with Raft River's disadvantage (higher maintenance costs), using “but” to show the trade-off: each system solves the other's problem but creates its own. Choices A and B each mention only one disadvantage, failing to compare. Choice D describes temperature capabilities without framing either as a disadvantage and omits maintenance costs entirely, so it does not compare the actual disadvantages at all.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
